questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Boron has a lower ionization energy compared to nitrogen because it has a partially filled 2p orbital, making it less stable and easier to remove an electron. Ionization energy increases across a period, and boron is located before nitrogen.

Nitrogen has a half-filled 2p orbital, which is particularly stable due to the absence of electron pair repulsion. This stability means that more energy is required to remove an electron, giving it a higher ionization energy.

Carbon's ionization energy is lower than nitrogen's because, like boron, it has a partially filled 2p orbital. This configuration is less stable, making it easier to remove an electron. Ionization energy increases across the period, and carbon is positioned before nitrogen.

Oxygen, despite being after nitrogen in the period, has a lower ionization energy. This is due to the presence of paired electrons in its 2p orbital, causing electron repulsion and making it easier to remove an electron compared to nitrogen's half-filled stable configuration.

questions.explanation: Nitrogen having a half-filled 2p orbital will have no electron pairs in the valence shell, so there will be no repulsion. Making nitrogen much more stable and hence requiring a greater amount of energy.

questions.text: The geometry of H2O is similar to that of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: MgCl2 has a linear geometry due to its two bonding pairs and no lone pairs around the central magnesium atom. In contrast, H2O has a bent geometry due to two lone pairs on oxygen, making this option incorrect.

SnCl2 has a V-shaped bent geometry due to the presence of one lone pair and two bonding pairs on the tin atom, similar to the bent geometry of H2O, which also has two lone pairs and two bonding pairs on oxygen. Hence, this option is correct.
HgCl2 has a linear geometry with two bonding pairs and no lone pairs around the central mercury atom, unlike the bent geometry of H2O. Therefore, this option is incorrect.
NH3 possesses a trigonal pyramidal geometry due to one lone pair and three bonding pairs around the nitrogen atom, which differs from the bent geometry of H2O. Thus, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Tin atoms have 4 electrons in their valence shell and two shared pairs thus having a V-shaped bent structure. The H2O molecule also has V-shaped bent structure. Hence, this option is correct.

questions.text: Which one of the following molecules does not follow the duplet or octet rule?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: NH3 follows the octet rule. Nitrogen has 5 valence electrons, so it needs 3 more to have an octet. It forms 3 single bonds with hydrogen atoms, giving each hydrogen atom 2 electrons and giving nitrogen 8 electrons.
HCl follows the octet rule. Hydrogen has 1 valence electron, so it needs 1 more to have an octet. It forms a single bond with a chlorine atom, giving each atom 8 electrons.

AlCl3 does not follow the octet rule. Aluminum has 3 valence electrons, so it needs 5 more to have an octet. It forms 3 single bonds with chlorine atoms, giving each chlorine atom 8 electrons but leaving aluminum with only 6 electrons. This is because aluminum is a small atom with high electronegativity, so it does not want to share too many electrons.

CCl4 follows the octet rule. Carbon has 4 valence electrons, so it needs 4 more to have an octet. It forms 4 single bonds with chlorine atoms, giving each chlorine atom 8 electrons and giving carbon 8 electrons.

questions.explanation: AlCl3 does not follow the octet rule. Aluminum has 3 valence electrons, so it needs 5 more to have an octet. It forms 3 single bonds with chlorine atoms, giving each chlorine atom 8 electrons but leaving aluminum with only 6 electrons. This is because aluminum is a small atom with high electronegativity, so it does not want to share too many electrons.

questions.text: In which of the following molecules would you expect the nitrogen-nitrogen bond to be the shortest?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: In hydrazine, the nitrogen-nitrogen bond is a single bond. Single bonds are generally longer than double or triple bonds, meaning the N-N bond in N2H4 is longer than in molecules with multiple bonds between nitrogen atoms.
Ammonia (NH3) does not contain any nitrogen-nitrogen bonds. It consists solely of N-H bonds, so it is irrelevant to determining the shortest N-N bond length.

Nitrogen gas (N2) has a nitrogen-nitrogen triple bond, which is the shortest type of covalent bond among single, double, and triple bonds. This triple bond results in a very strong and short bond, making N2 the molecule with the shortest N-N bond length among the options.
Nitrous oxide (N2O) contains a nitrogen-nitrogen double bond. Double bonds are shorter than single bonds but longer than triple bonds, making the N-N bond in N2O longer than in nitrogen gas (N2).

questions.explanation: The nitrogen-nitrogen (N-N) bond in N2 (nitrogen gas) would be the shortest among the given molecules. This is because N2 consists of two nitrogen atoms bonded together to form a diatomic molecule. This linear shape is due to the linear arrangement of the two sulfur atoms around the central carbon atom.Polarity: Although the individual carbon-sulfur bonds in CS2 are polar (with sulfur being more electronegative than carbon), the linear molecular geometry of CS2 results in the two polar bonds canceling each other out. questions.text: Energy of an atom in a compound is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: When atoms combine to form a compound, they often release energy in the form of chemical bonds. The energy required to break the individual atoms apart and form the compound is typically greater than the energy released when the atoms come together to form the compound. Therefore, the energy of the atom in a compound is generally lower than that of the individual atom.

If the energy of an atom in a compound was equal to that of the individual atom, there would be no energy change during the formation of the compound. However, energy is usually released during the formation of compounds, leading to a lower energy state for the atoms in the compound.

Therefore, the energy of the atom in a compound is generally lower than that of the individual atom.

It is generally predictable that the energy of an atom in a compound will be lower than that of the individual atom due to the formation of chemical bonds and energy release. While the exact energy value might depend on the specific atoms and the type of compound formed, the overall trend of lower energy in the compound is a predictable outcome.

questions.text: The bond will be perfectly covalent when the electronegativity difference of bonded atoms is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: When the electronegativity difference between bonded atoms is exactly 1.7, the bond is not perfectly covalent. It falls into the category of a polar covalent bond, where there is a partial transfer of electron density from one atom to another. This results in the formation of partial charges (dipoles) across the bond.

When the electronegativity difference is greater than 1.7, the bond is considered ionic rather than covalent. In an ionic bond, there is a complete transfer of electrons from one atom to another, resulting in the formation of ions with opposite charges.

This range indicates a polar covalent bond. The electronegativity difference is significant enough to create partial charges across the bond, but not so large as to result in a complete transfer of electrons. The atoms in the bond share electrons, but one atom will have a slightly stronger pull on the electrons, leading to an uneven distribution of electron density.

A covalent bond is formed when two atoms share electrons to achieve a more stable electron configuration. In a perfectly covalent bond, the electronegativity difference between the two bonded atoms is zero. Electronegativity is a measure of an element's ability to attract electrons in a chemical bond. When the electronegativity difference is zero, it means that the two atoms have the same electronegativity, and they share electrons equally, resulting in a purely covalent bond. For example, in a diatomic molecule like hydrogen (H2) or chlorine (Cl2), the two atoms have the same electronegativity, and the bond between them is perfectly covalent because they share electrons equally. As you move across the periodic table, electronegativity differences between elements increase, leading to more polar covalent and ionic bonds. So, a perfectly covalent bond occurs when there is no electronegativity difference between the bonded atoms.

questions.explanation: A covalent bond is formed when two atoms share electrons to achieve a more stable electron configuration. In a perfectly covalent bond, the electronegativity difference between the two bonded atoms is zero. Electronegativity is a measure of an element's ability to attract electrons in a chemical bond. As you move across the periodic table, electronegativity differences between elements increase, leading to more polar covalent and ionic bonds. So, a perfectly covalent bond occurs when there is no electronegativity difference between the bonded atoms.

questions.text: Mostly ionic compounds are produced between elements of group:
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.explanation: In ionic compounds, one element donates electrons (typically a metal or an element with low electronegativity), becoming a positively charged cation, while the other element accepts those electrons (typically a non-metal or an element with high electronegativity), becoming a negatively charged anion. The resulting electrostatic attraction between the cations and anions holds the compound together in a lattice structure. So, ionic compounds are indeed commonly produced when elements from Group IA, IIA, and VIIA are involved in chemical reactions due to the significant electronegativity differences between these groups of elements.

questions.text: Specie in which the central atom uses sp2 hybrid orbitals in its bonding is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: PH3 (Phosphine): The central atom is phosphorus (P). Phosphorus has 5 valence electrons. In PH3, phosphorus forms three sigma (σ) bonds with three hydrogen atoms. The electron configuration around phosphorus in PH3 is 1s² 2s² 2p³. To form three sigma bonds, phosphorus undergoes sp³ hybridization. Therefore, it uses sp³ hybrid orbitals.

NH3 (Ammonia): The central atom is nitrogen (N). Nitrogen has 5 valence electrons. In NH3, nitrogen forms three sigma (σ) bonds with three hydrogen atoms. The electron configuration around nitrogen in NH3 is 1s² 2s² 2p³. To form three sigma bonds, nitrogen undergoes sp³ hybridization. Therefore, it uses sp³ hybrid orbitals.

CH3+ (Methyl cation): The central atom is carbon (C). Carbon has 4 valence electrons. In CH3+, carbon forms three sigma (σ) bonds with three hydrogen atoms. The electron configuration around carbon in CH3+ is 1s² 2s² 2p². To form three sigma bonds, carbon undergoes sp² hybridization. Therefore, it uses sp² hybrid orbitals.
The specie in which the central atom uses sp2 hybrid orbitals in its bonding is CH3+. This option is incorrect.

questions.text: On the basis of VSEPR theory, a molecule with three bond pairs and no lone pair of electrons will have a structure:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: According to the VSEPR (Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion) theory, a molecule with three bond pairs and no lone pair of electrons will have a trigonal planar molecular structure.

According to the VSEPR (Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion) theory, a molecule with three bond pairs and no lone pair of electrons will have a trigonal planar molecular structure. In a trigonal planar arrangement, the bond angles between the three bond pairs are approximately 120 degrees. The electrons in the bond pairs arrange themselves as far apart as possible to minimize repulsion between them, leading to a planar geometry. An example of a molecule with this structure is boron trifluoride (BF3). In BF3, boron forms three sigma (σ) bonds with three fluorine atoms, resulting in a trigonal planar molecular geometry.

questions.text: Pi bonds are produced by overlapping of orbital's
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is correct because pi bonds are formed by the lateral overlap of unhybridized p-orbitals.
This is incorrect because hybrid orbitals do not participate in the formation of pi bonds; they are involved in sigma bonds.
This is incorrect because hybrid orbitals form sigma bonds through head-on overlapping, not pi bonds.
This is incorrect because s-orbitals overlap to form sigma bonds, not pi bonds, due to their spherical shape.

questions.explanation: Pi bonds are formed by the side-to-side overlap of two parallel unhybridized p-orbitals. These bonds are weaker than sigma bonds, which are formed by end-to-end overlap of orbitals. Hybrid orbitals, such as sp, sp2, or sp3, are involved in forming sigma bonds and are not capable of forming pi bonds. Similarly, s-orbitals contribute to sigma bonds due to their spherical nature, which supports direct overlap.

questions.text: Which of the following shows zero dipole moment:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: CH4 (methane) is a tetrahedral molecule with four identical C-H bonds. The C-H bonds are polar, but the individual dipole moments cancel each other out because they are arranged in a symmetrical tetrahedral shape.

NH3 (ammonia) is a trigonal pyramidal molecule with three identical N-H bonds. The N-H bonds are polar, and the individual dipole moments do not cancel each other out completely because the molecule is not symmetrical.
HF (hydrogen fluoride) is a linear molecule with one H-F bond. The H-F bond is polar, and the net dipole moment of HF points from H to F.

H2O (water) is a bent molecule with two identical H-O bonds. The H-O bonds are polar, and the individual dipole moments do not cancel each other out completely because the molecule is not symmetrical. The net dipole moment of H2O points from H to O.

questions.text: Polarity of a molecule is expressed in terms of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Bond strength is a measure of the force that holds two atoms together in a bond. The stronger the bond, the more difficult it is to break the bond. Bond strength is not a good measure of polarity because it does not take into account the distribution of electrons in the molecule.

Bond length is the distance between the nuclei of two atoms in a bond. The shorter the bond length, the stronger the bond. Bond length is not a good measure of polarity because it does not take into account the distribution of electrons in the molecule.

The polarity of a molecule is expressed in terms of dipole moment. Dipole moment is a measure of the separation of charge in a molecule. A molecule with a dipole moment has a positive end and a negative end. The strength of the dipole moment is measured in Debye (D).
Shape is the three-dimensional arrangement of the atoms in a molecule. The shape of a molecule can affect its polarity, but it is not the only factor.

questions.explanation: The polarity of a molecule is expressed in terms of its dipole moment. The dipole moment is a measure of the overall electrical charge separation within a molecule and its orientation. It indicates the degree of polarity in the molecule. A molecule is considered polar if it has a net dipole moment, meaning that there is an uneven distribution of electrons that results in a partial positive charge on one end of the molecule and a partial negative charge on the other end. This creates a separation of charge, or a "dipole," within the molecule.

questions.text: A covalent bond may be:

options.explanation: A covalent bond may be partially ionic or 100% covalent because the electrons that are shared between the two atoms may not be shared equally. In a 100% covalent bond, the electrons are shared equally between the two atoms. However, in a partially ionic bond, the electrons are shared unequally, with one atom having a slightly stronger pull on the electrons than the other atom.

Ionic bonds, on the other hand, are formed by the complete transfer of electrons from one atom to another. This means that there is no sharing of electrons in an ionic bond, and the electrons are completely transferred from one atom to the other.

questions.text: The hybridization of atomic orbitals of central atoms in CO2, BF3 and NH4+ respectively are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The hybridization of atomic orbitals of central atoms in CO2, BF3, and NH4+ are sp, sp2, and sp3, respectively. CO2 is a linear molecule with a triple bond between the carbon and oxygen atoms. The carbon atom is sp hybridized, which means that it has two sp orbitals. The two sp orbitals are used to form the two C=O bonds, and there are no lone pairs of electrons on the carbon atom. BF3 is a trigonal planar molecule with three single bonds between the boron and fluorine atoms. The boron atom is sp2 hybridized, which means that it has three sp2 orbitals. The three sp2 orbitals are used to form the three B-F bonds, and there is one lone pair of electrons on the boron atom. NH4+ is a tetrahedral molecule with four single bonds between the nitrogen and hydrogen atoms. The nitrogen atom is sp3 hybridized, which means that it has four sp3 orbitals. The four sp3 orbitals are used to form the four N-H bonds, and there are no lone pairs of electrons on the nitrogen atom.

questions.text: The shape of NH2- is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The NH2- ion has a bent V shape
The NH2- ion has a bent V shape

The shape of the NH2- ion (amide ion) is indeed V-shaped or bent. This shape is commonly referred to as "angular" or "bent." It is a result of the arrangement of the atoms and lone pairs of electrons around the central nitrogen atom. NH2- consists of one nitrogen atom (N) and two hydrogen atoms (H) bonded to the nitrogen atom. Additionally, there is one extra lone pair of electrons on the nitrogen atom. The presence of the lone pair affects the molecule's shape. The lone pair of electrons occupies more space than a bonding pair, causing the hydrogen atoms to be pushed closer together, resulting in a bent or V-shaped molecular geometry. The bond angle in NH2- is less than the ideal tetrahedral angle, which is 109.5 degrees. In the case of NH2-, the bond angle is approximately 104.5 degrees. This deviation from the ideal bond angle is due to the lone pair's electron repulsion, which compresses the bond angles, resulting in a V-shaped geometry.
The NH2- ion has a bent V shape

Option A is incorrect because sp2-s overlaps are characteristic of trigonal planar molecules, not water. Option B is incorrect as sp-s overlaps are found in linear molecules. Option C is incorrect because p-p overlaps do not occur in the bonding of H2O, which involves hybridized orbitals and s orbitals.

questions.text: Bond angle of H2S is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: While 90 degrees is a common angle for octahedral geometries, H2S doesn't have this geometry. The angle is influenced by lone pair repulsion.
104.5 degrees is the bond angle for water (H2O), which is similar to H2S but not the same due to differences in electronegativity and atomic size.
The bond angle of H2S is approximately 92 degrees due to the repulsion between lone pairs on sulfur, which slightly reduces the angle from the ideal tetrahedral angle of 109.5 degrees.
105.5 degrees is greater than the bond angle of H2S. This angle might suggest influences similar to those in H2O, but H2S has a larger central atom, reducing the angle.

questions.explanation: The bond angle of H2S is 92.1 degrees. This is because the sulfur atom is sp3 hybridized, and the three H-S bonds are arranged in a trigonal pyramidal configuration. The lone pair of electrons on the sulfur atom repels the H-S bonds, which causes the bond angle to be slightly less than 109.5 degrees, which is the ideal bond angle for sp3 hybridized atoms.

questions.text: Indicate the correct order for ionization energy:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: As you remove electrons from an atom, the remaining electrons are held more tightly to the nucleus. This is because the positive charge of the nucleus is not as effectively shielded by the remaining electrons.

As you remove electrons from an atom, the remaining electrons are held more tightly to the nucleus. This is because the positive charge of the nucleus is not as effectively shielded by the remaining electrons.

This is because it takes more energy to remove an electron from an atom that already has a positive charge. The first ionization energy (I.E1) is the energy required to remove the first electron from a neutral atom. The second ionization energy (I.E2) is the energy required to remove the second electron from a singly charged positive ion. The third ionization energy (I.E3) is the energy required to remove the third electron from a doubly charged positive ion.As you remove electrons from an atom, the remaining electrons are held more tightly to the nucleus. This is because the positive charge of the nucleus is not as effectively shielded by the remaining electrons.

questions.text: Which one is not an absolute term for the element?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ionization energy is an absolute term because it measures the energy required to remove an electron from an isolated atom, a fundamental property of each element.

Electronegativity is not an absolute term for an element. It is a relative measure of an element's ability to attract electrons when bonded to another element, often compared on scales like the Pauling scale.
Electron affinity is an absolute term as it quantifies the energy change when an electron is added to a neutral atom, reflecting a fundamental characteristic of the element.
Atomic size is an absolute term as it represents the physical dimension of an atom, which is a basic property of each element.

questions.explanation: Electronegativity is not an absolute term for an element. Electronegativity is a relative measure of an element's ability to attract electrons in a chemical bond when it is bonded to another element. It is typically expressed on the Pauling scale or other similar scales, and it provides a way to compare the relative electron-attracting power of different elements.Absolute terms for elements typically refer to their fundamental properties, such as atomic number, atomic mass, or the number of protons, neutrons, or electrons. Electronegativity, on the other hand, is a comparative measure that depends on the element's behavior in a chemical context and its interactions with other elements.

questions.text: The charge on a cation M is +2 & on anion A is -3, the compound formed has formula:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Write the charges of the cation and anion next to each other. Cross the charges, so that the positive charge of the cation is next to the negative charge of the anion, and vice versa. Write the numbers of each atom in the compound, so that the number of cations is equal to the number of anions. The formula of compound becomes M3A2

The compound formed has the formula M3A2. The cation M has a charge of +2, and the anion A has a charge of -3. To balance the charges, the compound must have three M cations for every two A anions. This gives the formula M3A2.

Write the charges of the cation and anion next to each other.Cross the charges, so that the positive charge of the cation is next to the negative charge of the anion, and vice versa.Write the numbers of each atom in the compound, so that the number of cations is equal to the number of anions. The formula of compound becomes M3A2

Write the charges of the cation and anion next to each other.Cross the charges, so that the positive charge of the cation is next to the negative charge of the anion, and vice versa.Write the numbers of each atom in the compound, so that the number of cations is equal to the number of anions. The formula of compound becomes M3A2

questions.explanation: The compound formed has the formula M3A2. The cation M has a charge of +2, and the anion A has a charge of -3. To balance the charges, the compound must have three M cations for every two A anions. This gives the formula M3A2

questions.text: Which of the following molecule has highest dipole moment:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: SO3 has a zero dipole moment. The three S-O bonds are arranged in a triangular planar configuration, so they cancel each other out.
In SnCl2, both the Sn-Cl bonds are angular with each other. Thus, the dipole moment of one Sn-Cl bond cannot be cancelled by each other and a permanent dipole moment is observed in this molecule.

CO2 has a zero dipole moment because the molecule is linear. The two C-O bonds are arranged in a straight line, and the electronegativity of carbon and oxygen are similar. This means that the partial charges on the carbon and oxygen atoms are equal in magnitude and opposite in direction, so they cancel each other out.

BF3 has a zero dipole moment because the molecule is symmetrical. The three B-F bonds are arranged in a trigonal planar configuration, and the electronegativity of boron and fluorine are similar. This means that the partial charges on the boron and fluorine atoms are equal in magnitude and opposite in direction, so they cancel each other out.

questions.explanation: In SnCl2, both the Sn-Cl bonds are angular with each other. Thus, the dipole moment of one Sn-Cl bond cannot be cancelled by each other and a permanent dipole moment is observed in this molecule.

questions.text: Which compound is more ionic?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: HF has a difference in electronegativity of 1.9, which makes it a polar covalent compound rather than ionic.
CsCl has the largest difference in electronegativity between cesium and chlorine at 3.22, making it the most ionic compound among the given options.
NaCl has a difference in electronegativity of 2.16, which indicates a significant ionic character, but it is less ionic than CsCl.
H2O has a difference in electronegativity of 1.22, which results in a polar covalent bond rather than an ionic bond.

questions.explanation: CsCl is the most ionic compound out of the four listed. The other three compounds, HF, NaCl, and H2O, are all polar covalent compounds.The ionic character of a bond is determined by the difference in electronegativity between the two atoms that are bonded together. A larger difference in electronegativity means that the bond is more ionic. In CsCl, the difference in electronegativity between cesium and chlorine is 3.22. This is the largest difference in electronegativity out of the four compounds, so CsCl is the most ionic.

questions.text: An ionic corn ound A+B- is most likel to be formed when:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: When element A has a high electronegativity and element B has a low ionization energy.
options.explanation: Incorrect. High electronegativity suggests a tendency to attract electrons, which doesn't favor the formation of a positive ion.

options.text: When element A has a low ionization energy and element B has a high electron affinity.
options.explanation: Correct. Element A easily loses an electron to form a positive ion, and element B readily gains an electron to form a negative ion, favoring ionic bond formation.

options.text: When both elements have high ionization energies.
options.explanation: Incorrect. High ionization energies imply that neither element easily loses electrons, which is not conducive to forming an ionic bond.

options.text: When both elements have low electron affinities.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Low electron affinity means that an element is less likely to gain electrons, which does not favor the formation of a negative ion.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Ionic compounds typically form when one element (usually a metal) can easily lose an electron, indicated by low ionization energy, to become a positive ion (cation), and another element (usually a non-metal) has high electron affinity, allowing it to easily gain an electron to become a negative ion (anion). Option A is incorrect because high electronegativity means that element A would attract electrons rather than lose them. Option C is incorrect because high ionization energies for both elements mean neither will readily lose electrons. Option D is incorrect because low electron affinities for both elements mean they are unlikely to gain electrons, which is necessary for forming anions in ionic compounds.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a ionic solid?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Sugar C12H22O11(s) is a molecular solid made up of sugar molecules. The sugar molecules are held together by intermolecular forces, such as hydrogen bonds.
Graphite C(s) is a molecular solid made up of carbon atoms. The carbon atoms are held together by covalent bonds, but the layers of carbon atoms are held together by weak van der Waals forces.
Carbon dioxide CO2(s) is a molecular solid made up of carbon dioxide molecules. The carbon dioxide molecules are held together by covalent bonds.

Ionic solids are formed when positively charged ions (cations) and negatively charged ions (anions) are attracted to each other. The cations and anions are held together by electrostatic forces. Magnesium fluoride is an ionic solid because it is made up of magnesium cations (Mg2+) and fluoride anions (F-). The magnesium cations and fluoride anions are held together by strong electrostatic forces.The other substances in the list are not ionic solids because they are not made up of cations and anions. They are all molecular solids, which are held together by covalent bonds or weak intermolecular forces.

questions.explanation: Ionic solids are formed when positively charged ions (cations) and negatively charged ions (anions) are attracted to each other. The cations and anions are held together by electrostatic forces. Magnesium fluoride is an ionic solid because it is made up of magnesium cations (Mg2+) and fluoride anions (F-). The magnesium cations and fluoride anions are held together by strong electrostatic forces.The other substances in the list are not ionic solids because they are not made up of cations and anions. They are all molecular solids, which are held together by covalent bonds or weak intermolecular forces.

questions.text: All are true for pie-bond except:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: pie-bond is formed from sp hybrid orbitals.
options.explanation: The statement that "pie-bond is formed from sp hybrid orbitals" is not true. A pie-bond is formed by the parallel overlap of unhybridized p orbitals. The s orbitals are not involved in the formation of pie-bonds.

options.text: pie-bond is formed by the parallel overlap of p-orbitals.
options.explanation: Pie-bond is formed by the parallel overlapping of p orbitals.

options.text: pie-bond is formed when as sigma bond is already present.
options.explanation: A pie-bond is formed when a sigma bond is already present.

options.text: pie-bond is weaker than sigma bond.
options.explanation: It is weaker than a sigma bond.

questions.explanation: The statement that "pie-bond is formed from sp hybrid orbitals" is not true. A pie-bond is formed by the parallel overlap of unhybridized p orbitals. The s orbitals are not involved in the formation of pie-bonds.

questions.text: Which one of the following molecules has zero dipole moment.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: NH3 has a non-zero dipole moment because the nitrogen atom is more electronegative than the hydrogen atoms, so the electrons in the N-H bonds are more likely to be found closer to the nitrogen atom. This creates a partial negative charge on the nitrogen atom and a partial positive charge on the hydrogen atoms.

NF3 also has a non-zero dipole moment because the nitrogen atom is more electronegative than the fluorine atoms. However, the difference in electronegativity between nitrogen and fluorine is smaller than the difference in electronegativity between nitrogen and hydrogen, so the dipole moment of NF3 is smaller than the dipole moment of NH3.

The dipole moment of BF3 is zero because the molecule is symmetrical. The three B-F bonds are arranged in a trigonal planar configuration, and the electronegativity of boron and fluorine are similar. This means that the partial charges on the boron and fluorine atoms are equal in magnitude and opposite in direction, so they cancel each other out.

H2O has a non-zero dipole moment because the oxygen atom is more electronegative than the hydrogen atoms. The two O-H bonds are not symmetrical, so the dipole moments of the two bonds do not cancel each other out.

questions.explanation: The dipole moment of BF3 is zero because the molecule is symmetrical. The three B-F bonds are arranged in a trigonal planar configuration, and the electronegativity of boron and fluorine are similar. This means that the partial charges on the boron and fluorine atoms are equal in magnitude and opposite in direction, so they cancel each other out.

questions.text: The bond angle in H2O is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This angle is typical for square planar structures, not for the bent shape of water.
This angle is typical for tetrahedral structures, but the presence of lone pairs on water reduces it.
A linear structure would have this bond angle, but water is bent due to lone pairs.
Water has a bent shape with two lone pairs, resulting in a bond angle of 104.5 degrees according to VSEPR theory.

questions.explanation: The bond angle in H2O is 104.5 due to presence of two lone pair of electrons.This fact can be explained by the help of VSEPR.

questions.text: Which of the following has no tendency to form coordinate covalent bond with H+?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Incorrect. NH3 has a lone pair of electrons on the nitrogen atom, which can be donated to form a coordinate covalent bond with H+.
Incorrect. H2O has two lone pairs of electrons on the oxygen atom, which can be used to form a coordinate covalent bond with H+.
Correct. BF3 does not have a lone pair of electrons to donate for forming a coordinate covalent bond with H+. Boron in BF3 is electron-deficient and tends to accept electrons rather than donate them.
Incorrect. CH3OH has a lone pair of electrons on the oxygen atom, which can be used to form a coordinate covalent bond with H+.

questions.explanation: This is true because octet of B is not complete in BF3. they accept a pair of electrons from a donor and form a coordinate covalent bond.

questions.text: Which one is non — polar although it contains polar bonds?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: HCl is a polar molecule because the chlorine atom is more electronegative than hydrogen, leading to an uneven electron distribution.
H2O is a polar molecule due to its bent shape, which causes an uneven distribution of charge.
CO2 is a nonpolar molecule despite having polar bonds. Its linear structure allows the bond dipoles to cancel out, resulting in no net dipole moment.
SO2 is a polar molecule due to its bent shape, which results in an uneven distribution of charge.

questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide: CO2 has polar bonds but is a nonpolar molecule. The structure of CO2 is linear. The individual bond dipoles cancel each other as they point in opposite direction and are equal in magnitude.

questions.text: When 's' character of hybridized orbitals decreases the bond angle:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: As the 's' character decreases in hybrid orbitals, the bond angles decrease. This is because orbitals with less 's' character have more 'p' character, which forms wider angles.
This is incorrect. Increasing the 's' character actually increases the bond angle. Less 's' character results in more 'p' character, which means smaller bond angles.
Bond angles cannot become zero due to changes in orbital hybridization. This option is conceptually incorrect.
Bond angles are affected by changes in hybridization, so this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that the bond angle decreases. Hybrid orbitals with higher 's' character (e.g., sp) have larger bond angles (180°) compared to those with more 'p' character (e.g., sp3 with 109.5°). As 's' character decreases, the proportion of 'p' character increases, leading to smaller bond angles. Options B, C, and D are incorrect because they either misunderstand the relationship between 's' character and bond angles or suggest impossible outcomes.

questions.text: A chemical bond is formed to:1. attain the octet state2. minimize energy3. gain stability4. decrease reactivityWhich statements ac correct.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is incorrect because, while attaining the octet state and minimizing energy are reasons for bond formation, gaining stability and decreasing reactivity are additional factors.
This is correct because a chemical bond is formed to attain the octet state, minimize energy, gain stability, and decrease reactivity.
This is incorrect because, in addition to attaining the octet state, minimizing energy, and gaining stability, decreasing reactivity is also a reason for bond formation.
This is incorrect because, while minimizing energy, gaining stability, and decreasing reactivity are reasons for bond formation, attaining the octet state is also a key factor.

questions.explanation: A chemical bond is formed to achieve several goals: 1) Attaining the octet state helps atoms reach a stable electronic configuration similar to noble gases. 2) Minimizing energy is achieved by forming bonds, which releases energy and makes the system more stable. 3) Gaining stability is a consequence of achieving a lower energy state. 4) Decreasing reactivity occurs because a stable, low-energy state is less likely to react further. Therefore, all the given statements are correct, making Option B the correct choice. The other options are incorrect because they omit one or more reasons for bond formation.

questions.text: Which one of the following molecules is not planar?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Benzene is a planar molecule with a hexagonal ring structure, where all carbon atoms are sp2 hybridized.
Phosphorus trichloride (PCl3) has a trigonal pyramidal shape due to the presence of a lone pair on the phosphorus atom, making it non-planar.
Ethene (C2H4) is a planar molecule because it contains a double bond between carbon atoms, resulting in a trigonal planar geometry.
Boron trifluoride (BF3) is a planar molecule with a trigonal planar geometry, where boron is sp2 hybridized.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Phosphorus trichloride (PCl3) because it has a trigonal pyramidal geometry, which results from the lone pair on the phosphorus atom causing a deviation from planarity. In contrast, Benzene, Ethene, and Boron trifluoride are all planar molecules. Benzene has a hexagonal planar ring, Ethene has a planar double-bonded structure, and Boron trifluoride has a trigonal planar arrangement.

questions.text: The ability of an atom in a covalent bond to attract the bonding electrons is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ionization energy is the energy required to remove an electron from an atom, which is different from attracting electrons in a bond.
Ionic bond energy pertains to the energy involved in the attraction between ions, not the attraction of electrons in a covalent bond.
Electronegativity is the measure of an atom's ability to attract and hold onto electrons in a covalent bond, making it the correct answer.
Electron affinity involves the energy change that occurs when an electron is added to an atom, not its ability to attract bonding electrons.

questions.explanation: Electronegativity is the correct answer because it describes the tendency of an atom to attract and hold onto shared electrons in a covalent bond. Ionization energy and electron affinity relate to energy changes involving electrons but do not describe this attraction in a bond. Ionic bond energy refers to ionic interactions, not covalent bonds.

questions.text: The angle between unhybridize p-orbital and three sp2 hybrid orbitals of each carbon atom in ethene is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is incorrect. The unhybridized p-orbital in ethene is perpendicular to the sp2 hybrid orbitals, indicating a 90-degree angle.
Incorrect for this scenario. 120 degrees is the angle between the sp2 hybrid orbitals themselves, not between them and the unhybridized p-orbital.
This is incorrect. 109.5 degrees is characteristic of sp3 hybridization, not sp2.
This is incorrect. 180 degrees is the angle associated with linear geometries, not the geometry of ethene.

questions.explanation: In ethene, each carbon atom undergoes sp2 hybridization, forming three sp2 hybrid orbitals that are arranged in a trigonal planar configuration with 120-degree angles between them. The unhybridized p-orbital, which participates in pi-bonding, is perpendicular to this plane, forming a 90-degree angle with the sp2 orbitals. Hence, the correct answer is 90 degrees. The other options are incorrect because they describe angles related to different geometries or hybridizations: 120 degrees applies to the angles between sp2 hybrid orbitals, 109.5 degrees to sp3 hybridized atoms, and 180 degrees to linear arrangements.

questions.text: In ‘H-F’ bond electronegativity difference is ‘1.9’. What is the type of this bond?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Fluorine has a high electronegativity of 4.0, whereas hydrogen has an electronegativity of 2.1. The significant difference of 1.9 in their electronegativities leads to an unequal sharing of electrons, with the electron pair being more attracted to fluorine, creating a polar covalent bond.

This is incorrect. Non-polar covalent bonds occur when the electronegativity difference is minimal (less than 0.5), resulting in equal sharing of electrons. In the H-F bond, the difference is 1.9, which causes a polar covalent bond.
Pi bonds involve the side-to-side overlap of p orbitals, found in multiple bonds (double or triple). The H-F bond is a single sigma bond, not a pi bond.
In a coordinate covalent bond, one atom donates both electrons in the bond. The H-F bond involves mutual sharing between hydrogen and fluorine, not donation of electrons by one atom.

questions.explanation: Fluorine is the most electronegative element with an electronegativity of 4.0 on the Pauling scale, while hydrogen has an electronegativity of 2.1. Due to this significant difference in electronegativity, the electron pair in the H-F bond is pulled towards fluorine, creating a partial negative charge on fluorine and a partial positive charge on hydrogen. Thus creating a polar covalent bond.

questions.text: When the two partially filled atomic orbitals overlap in such a way that the probability of finding electron is maximum around the line joining the two nuclei, the result is the formation of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is correct. A sigma bond is the strongest type of covalent bond, formed by the head-on overlap of atomic orbitals along the line joining the two nuclei.
This is incorrect. A pi bond is formed by the lateral or side-to-side overlap of atomic orbitals, not along the line joining the nuclei.
This is incorrect. A hydrogen bond is a type of dipole-dipole attraction between molecules, not a covalent bond formed by orbital overlap.
This is incorrect. A metallic bond involves the electrostatic attraction between conduction electrons and positively charged metal ions, not the overlap of atomic orbitals.

questions.explanation: When the two partially filled atomic orbitals overlap in such a way that the probability of finding electron is maximum around the line joining the two nuclei, the result is the formation of sigma bond.

questions.text: Electron affinity of the atom is the energy released when:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Electron is added to a gaseous atom.
options.explanation: This is correct. The electron affinity of an atom is the energy released when an electron is added to an isolated gaseous atom, forming an anion with a unit negative charge.

options.text: A covalent bond in a molecule is broken.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Breaking a covalent bond requires energy, whereas electron affinity involves the release of energy.

options.text: An electron is removed from a gaseous atom.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Removing an electron from a gaseous atom requires energy input, which is described by ionization energy, not electron affinity.

options.text: A covalent bond is formed between atoms.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. While forming a covalent bond releases energy, it involves interactions between atoms, not the addition of an electron to a single atom as in electron affinity.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that electron affinity refers to the energy released when an electron is added to a gaseous atom, resulting in the formation of an anion. This is a key concept in understanding how atoms interact with electrons. Option A is correct because it directly describes this process. Option B is incorrect as it involves breaking bonds, which consumes energy. Option C is incorrect as it describes ionization energy, which is the energy required to remove an electron. Option D is incorrect because forming a covalent bond involves two atoms sharing electrons, which is a different process.

questions.text: Liquid oxygen is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Diamagnetic substances are repelled by a magnetic field. Since liquid oxygen is attracted to a magnetic field, it cannot be diamagnetic.
Paramagnetic substances are attracted to magnetic fields. Liquid oxygen is paramagnetic because it has unpaired electrons in its molecular structure, which aligns in the presence of a magnetic field.
Antimagnetic is not a standard term used in chemistry or physics to describe magnetic properties; hence, it is not applicable to the nature of liquid oxygen.
Ferromagnetic substances can be permanently magnetized due to their domain structures. Liquid oxygen does not exhibit this property, as it only temporarily aligns with a magnetic field.

questions.explanation: Liquid oxygen is paramagnetic because it contains unpaired electrons, which cause it to be attracted to a magnetic field. This property is due to the presence of two unpaired electrons in its molecular orbital configuration. In contrast, diamagnetic substances have all paired electrons and are repelled by magnetic fields. Ferromagnetic materials, like iron, have domains that align in a magnetic field and can retain magnetization, which is not the case for liquid oxygen. 'Antimagnetic' is not a recognized term in describing magnetic properties, thus irrelevant in this context.

questions.text: In carbon dioxide (CO2) molecule, the number of sigma covalent bond(s) are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A carbon dioxide molecule consists of one carbon atom double bonded to two oxygen atoms. Each double bond contains one sigma bond and one pi bond, so there cannot be only one sigma bond in the entire molecule.
Each C=O double bond in carbon dioxide consists of one sigma bond and one pi bond. With two double bonds present, there are a total of two sigma bonds in the molecule.
Each double bond between carbon and oxygen consists of only one sigma bond. Therefore, three sigma bonds in CO2 is not possible.
Four sigma bonds cannot occur in CO2 because each of the two carbon-oxygen double bonds contains only one sigma bond.

questions.explanation: In a carbon dioxide (CO2) molecule, the carbon atom forms two double bonds, one with each oxygen atom. Each C=O double bond consists of one sigma bond and one pi bond. Therefore, in the entire molecule, there are two sigma bonds. Option B: 2 is the correct answer. Options A, C, and D are incorrect because they suggest an incorrect number of sigma bonds based on the structure of CO2.

questions.text: H2S has a net dipole moment while BeF2 has zero dipole moment, because of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: H2S molecule is linear, while BeF2 is angular.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. H2S is actually angular due to its bent shape, and BeF2 is linear.

options.text: H2S molecule is angular, while BeF2 molecule is linear.
options.explanation: This is correct. H2S has a bent shape causing a net dipole moment, whereas BeF2 is linear and symmetrical, resulting in zero net dipole moment.
While fluorine is more electronegative, this does not directly explain the dipole moment difference. The shape of the molecule is more relevant here.

options.text: Be is more electronegative than S.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Sulfur is more electronegative than beryllium, but the key factor is the molecular geometry, not electronegativity differences alone.

questions.explanation: The molecular geometry determines the net dipole moment of a molecule. H2S has a bent shape due to the presence of lone pairs on the sulfur atom, leading to a net dipole moment. On the other hand, BeF2 is linear and symmetrical, which causes the individual bond dipoles to cancel out, resulting in a net dipole moment of zero. Options A, C, and D are incorrect because they either describe the wrong molecular shapes or focus on electronegativity differences, which are not the primary reason for the observed dipole moments.

questions.text: Octet rule is not allowed in the formation of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: NF3 follows the octet rule as nitrogen has 5 valence electrons and forms three bonds with fluorine, completing its octet.
CCl4 obeys the octet rule. Carbon has four valence electrons and forms four bonds with chlorine, completing its octet.
CF4 also follows the octet rule. Carbon forms four bonds with fluorine, completing its octet.
PCl5 does not obey the octet rule because phosphorus can expand its valence shell to accommodate 10 electrons, forming five bonds with chlorine.

questions.explanation: The octet rule states that atoms tend to form bonds until they have eight electrons in their valence shell. However, elements in period 3 and beyond can expand their valence shells. PCl5 is an example where phosphorus, a period 3 element, forms five bonds with chlorine, resulting in 10 electrons in its valence shell, thus not following the octet rule. In contrast, NF3, CCl4, and CF4 all conform to the octet rule, with their central atoms achieving an octet through their respective bonding arrangements.

questions.text: The ionization energy is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: increases from left to right in a period
options.explanation: Correct! Ionization energy generally increases across a period from left to right due to increasing nuclear charge.

options.text: increases from top to bottom in a group
options.explanation: Incorrect. Ionization energy generally decreases down a group because the outer electrons are further from the nucleus and more shielded by inner electrons.

options.text: does not change in a period
options.explanation: Incorrect. Ionization energy does change within a period, typically increasing as you move from left to right.

options.text: does not change in a group
options.explanation: Incorrect. Ionization energy changes as you move down a group, usually decreasing due to increased electron shielding.

questions.explanation: Ionization energy is the energy required to remove an electron from an atom in the gaseous state. As you move from left to right across a period, the number of protons in the nucleus increases, creating a greater attractive force on the electrons. This results in an increase in ionization energy. Conversely, as you move down a group, the outer electrons are further from the nucleus and experience more shielding from inner electron shells, which decreases the ionization energy. Therefore, the correct answer is that ionization energy increases from left to right in a period. The other options are incorrect because they either suggest ionization energy remains constant or incorrectly describe its trend across a group.

questions.text: In a period the atomic radii:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: As you move across a period, the atomic number increases, leading to a stronger pull on the electrons by the nucleus, which actually causes the atomic radii to decrease, not increase.
Atomic radii do not remain the same across a period because the increasing nuclear charge pulls electrons closer, reducing the size.

As you move across a period, the number of protons in the nucleus increases, enhancing the positive charge. This greater nuclear charge pulls the electrons closer to the nucleus, leading to a decrease in atomic radii.
Atomic radii consistently decrease across a period due to the increasing effective nuclear charge; they do not increase at any point.

questions.explanation: Along the period, the number of shells remain same and the attraction of nucleus towards outermost electrons increase with increase in proton number. Therefore, size of atom decreases.

questions.text: In a group, the atomic radii:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The atomic radii increase as you move down a group because each successive element has an additional electron shell, making the atom larger.
If the atomic radii did not change, it would imply no additional electron shells are added, which is incorrect as each new period adds a new shell.
The atomic radii do not decrease down a group; they increase due to the addition of electron shells, which outweighs the increase in nuclear charge.
Within a group, the trend is not variable but consistent: atomic radii increase due to additional electron shells.

questions.explanation: An atom gets larger as the number of electronic shells increase; therefore the radius of atoms increases as you go down a certain group in the periodic table of elements.

questions.text: Energy required to remove electron from an atom:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ionization energy: The energy required to remove the most loosely bound electron from a neutral atom in its gaseous state, forming a cation.
Electropositivity is the tendency of an atom to lose electrons and form positively charged ions. It does not specifically refer to the energy required to remove an electron.
Electronegativity is the ability of an atom to attract shared electrons in a chemical bond, not the energy required to remove an electron.
Electron affinity refers to the energy change when an electron is added to a neutral atom, forming an anion, not when an electron is removed.

questions.explanation: Ionization energy: The amount of energy required to remove the most loosly bonded electron,valance electron to form a cation.

questions.text: Ionic bond is produced after complete transfer of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The nucleus is not involved in the formation of ionic bonds; it remains intact within the atom.

An ionic bond is formed through the transfer of one or more valence electrons from one element to another, typically from a metal to a non-metal. This transfer results in the formation of positively and negatively charged ions that attract each other.
Neutrons do not participate in chemical bonding as they are neutral particles and remain within the nucleus.
Protons cannot be transferred during chemical bonding as they are part of the atom's nucleus and define the element.

questions.explanation: An ionic bond is formed through the transfer of one or more valence electrons from one element to another. The transfer of electrons mainly occurs from a metal to a non-metal.

questions.text: Elements of group IA and IIA are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Electronegative elements have a strong tendency to attract electrons when forming chemical bonds. They are typically found on the right side of the periodic table, such as fluorine (F) and oxygen (O). Elements in Group IA and IIA are more likely to lose electrons than to gain them.

Electropositive elements tend to lose electrons easily when forming chemical bonds. This property is typical of elements in Groups IA (alkali metals) and IIA (alkaline earth metals), such as lithium (Li), sodium (Na), and calcium (Ca), making them electropositive.

While elements in their elemental state are electrically neutral, this option does not describe their chemical behavior, specifically their tendency to lose or gain electrons. Thus, it does not apply to the chemical properties of Group IA and IIA elements.

Non-metals are generally found on the right side of the periodic table and have properties opposite to metals. Elements in Groups IA and IIA are metals, not non-metals, and exhibit metallic properties.

questions.explanation: An element is considered electropositive if it has a tendency to lose electrons when forming chemical bonds. Electropositivity generally increases as you move down a group on the periodic table. Elements that are electropositive include the alkali metals (Group IA) and alkaline earth metals (Group IIA), such as sodium (Na), potassium (K), calcium (Ca), and magnesium (Mg). These elements tend to lose electrons to achieve a stable electron configuration.

questions.text: Which one has the least bond angle:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ammonia (NH3) has a bond angle of approximately 107 degrees. This angle is less than the ideal tetrahedral angle of 109.5 degrees due to the lone pair of electrons on the nitrogen atom, which creates electron repulsion and slightly compresses the bond angles.

Water (H2O) has a bond angle of approximately 104.5 degrees. The two lone pairs of electrons on the oxygen atom result in significant repulsion, leading to a compression of the bond angle from the ideal tetrahedral angle.
Methane (CH4) has a bond angle of 109.5 degrees, forming a perfect tetrahedral shape. It does not have any lone pairs to distort the bond angles.
Boron trifluoride (BF3) has a bond angle of approximately 120 degrees. It forms a trigonal planar geometry, resulting in larger bond angles compared to tetrahedral structures.

questions.explanation: Water (H2O) has a bond angle of approximately 104.5 degrees. Like ammonia, water has two lone pairs of electrons on the oxygen atom, causing a distortion in the bond angles from the ideal tetrahedral angle.

questions.text: The bond order in 02 molecule is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: O2 has a bond order greater than zero, indicating that it is a stable molecule with a bond between the two oxygen atoms.
While a bond order of 1 would indicate a single bond, O2 has a higher bond order due to the presence of both bonding and antibonding electrons in its molecular orbitals.

The bond order in an O2 (oxygen) molecule is 2. Bond order is determined by the difference between the number of bonding electrons and antibonding electrons, divided by 2. In O2, there are 10 bonding electrons and 6 antibonding electrons, resulting in a bond order of 2, indicating a double bond.
A bond order of 3 would suggest a triple bond, which is not the case for O2. O2 has a bond order of 2, representing a double bond.

questions.explanation: The bond order in an O2 (oxygen) molecule is 2.Bond order is a concept used in molecular orbital theory to describe the stability of a chemical bond between two atoms. It is calculated as the difference between the number of bonding electrons and the number of antibonding electrons divided by 2.

questions.text: Total number of valence electrons in phosphonium Ion ( PH ) is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Correct. The phosphonium ion (PH4+) has a total of 8 valence electrons. Phosphorus (P) initially has 5 valence electrons. However, due to the positive charge of +1 (PH4+), it loses one electron. Therefore, the total is 5 (from phosphorus) + 4 (from four hydrogen atoms) - 1 (lost electron) = 8 valence electrons.
This is incorrect. If the phosphonium ion had 10 valence electrons, it would imply an incorrect calculation of the charge or number of hydrogen atoms.
This is incorrect. A total of 9 valence electrons would suggest an error in accounting for the charge or number of hydrogen atoms.
This is incorrect. 12 valence electrons would require additional electrons beyond what is present in the phosphonium ion structure.

questions.explanation: The phosphonium ion (PH4+) has a total of 8 valence electrons.Phosphorus (P) is in Group 15 (also known as Group V or Group 5A) of the periodic table. It has 5 valence electrons. Since the ion has a positive charge of +1 (PH4+), it has lost one electron.So, the total number of valence electrons in the phosphonium ion (PH4+) is 5 (from phosphorus) + 3 (from three hydrogen atoms) - 1 (lost electron) = 8 valence electrons.

questions.text: MTBE is part of petrol:What are approximate values of angle P and angle Q in a molecule of MTBE?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Incorrect. A 90° angle is not typical for the carbon-oxygen bond in MTBE, as it does not follow tetrahedral geometry.

Correct. The carbon atoms are sp3 hybridized, resulting in a bond angle of approximately 109.5° for angle P, consistent with tetrahedral geometry. Angle Q is approximately 105° typical for the C-O-C bond in ethers.
Incorrect. A 180° angle would imply a linear geometry, which is not applicable to MTBE's structure.
Incorrect. While 109.5° is a plausible angle for sp3 hybridized carbon, 180° suggests a linear geometry not present in MTBE.

questions.explanation: The carbon atoms (C) are also sp3 hybridized. The angle between the two adjacent carbon atoms and the oxygen atom is also approximately 109.5 degrees, following the tetrahedral geometry.

questions.text: Which of the following molecule has trigonal planar shape?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ammonia (NH3) has a trigonal pyramidal shape due to the presence of a lone pair on the nitrogen atom, which repels the bonding pairs of electrons.
Phosphorus trichloride (PCl3) has a trigonal pyramidal shape similar to ammonia, due to a lone pair on the phosphorus atom.
Boron trifluoride (BF3) has a trigonal planar shape because boron has three bonding pairs and no lone pairs, leading to an arrangement that minimizes electron pair repulsion in a plane.
Phosphorus pentafluoride (PF5) has a trigonal bipyramidal shape due to the presence of five bonding pairs around the phosphorus atom.

questions.explanation: The molecule BF3 has a trigonal planar shape. This is because the boron atom in BF3 forms three single bonds with fluorine atoms and has no lone pairs of electrons. The three bonding pairs of electrons are arranged in a plane at 120 degrees to each other, minimizing repulsion and resulting in a trigonal planar geometry.Ammonia (NH3) and phosphorus trichloride (PCl3) both have a trigonal pyramidal shape due to the presence of one lone pair of electrons that creates an asymmetrical distribution of electron density. Phosphorus pentafluoride (PF5) has a trigonal bipyramidal shape, which is different from trigonal planar because it involves five bonding pairs arranged in a three-dimensional structure.

questions.text: The unhybridized "p" orbital in sp2 hybridization is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The unhybridized "p" orbital in sp2 hybridization cannot be parallel to the sp2 orbitals, as it must be oriented differently.

options.text: In the same plane as sp2 orbitals
options.explanation: The unhybridized "p" orbital cannot be in the same plane as the sp2 orbitals; it has a different spatial orientation.
Correct. In sp2 hybridization, the unhybridized "p" orbital is perpendicular to the plane formed by the sp2 hybrid orbitals.

options.text: Out of the plane of sp2 orbitals
options.explanation: While the term "out of plane" might seem correct, it is not precise enough to describe the perpendicular orientation of the unhybridized "p" orbital.

questions.explanation: In sp2 hybridization, the unhybridized "p" orbital is perpendicular to the plane formed by the three sp2 hybrid orbitals. The three sp2 hybrid orbitals lie in the same plane, forming an angle of approximately 120 degrees with each other. The unhybridized "p" orbital is oriented at a right angle to this plane.

questions.text: All are Lewis acids except:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: BF3 is a Lewis acid because the boron atom has an empty 'p' orbital, allowing it to accept an electron pair from a Lewis base.
H2O is not a Lewis acid. It acts as a Lewis base due to its ability to donate a pair of electrons from the oxygen atom, forming a coordinate bond with a Lewis acid.
AlCl3 is a Lewis acid. The aluminum atom can accept an electron pair, fulfilling the role of an electron pair acceptor.
FeCl3 is a Lewis acid as the iron atom can accept an electron pair, classifying it as an electron pair acceptor.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is H2O. Water is a Lewis base because it has lone pairs of electrons on the oxygen atom that can be donated to form a bond with a Lewis acid. In contrast, BF3, AlCl3, and FeCl3 are all Lewis acids as they can accept electron pairs. BF3 has an empty p orbital on boron, AlCl3 can accept electrons due to its electron-deficient aluminum atom, and FeCl3 can accept electron pairs through its iron atom.

questions.text: Which of the following compound has sp3 hybridization:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: PCl5 has sp3d hybridization. In this molecule, phosphorus (P) utilizes one 3s, three 3p, and one 3d orbital to form five equivalent sp3d hybrid orbitals, which are responsible for forming bonds with the five chlorine atoms. This results in a trigonal bipyramidal structure.

SF6 exhibits sp3d2 hybridization. The sulfur (S) atom uses one 3s, three 3p, and two 3d orbitals to generate six equivalent sp3d2 hybrid orbitals, which form bonds with six fluorine atoms, resulting in an octahedral geometry.

NH3 has sp3 hybridization. The nitrogen (N) atom in ammonia forms three sigma bonds with hydrogen atoms and has one lone pair, utilizing one 2s and three 2p orbitals to form four sp3 hybrid orbitals. This gives ammonia a tetrahedral electron geometry with a trigonal pyramidal shape due to the lone pair.

HN=NH, also known as hydrazine, is incorrectly described here. The correct formula for hydrazine is N2H4, which involves sp3 hybridization for each nitrogen atom, forming sigma bonds with hydrogen and the other nitrogen atom. The lone pairs on nitrogen occupy the remaining sp3 hybrid orbitals.

questions.explanation: NH3 has sp3 hybridization. In ammonia, the central nitrogen (N) atom forms three sigma bonds with three hydrogen atoms. The nitrogen's 2s and two of its 2p orbitals hybridize to form four sp3 hybrid orbitals, one of which contains a lone pair of electrons.

questions.text: The chemical species having smallest bond angle:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: NH3 has a bond angle of approximately 107 degrees. The central nitrogen (N) atom is bonded to three hydrogen (H) atoms, and the lone pair of electrons on nitrogen causes a slight compression of the bond angles from the ideal tetrahedral angle of 109.5 degrees.

NCl3 has a bond angle of about 107 degrees. The presence of larger chlorine (Cl) atoms compared to hydrogen in NH3 and NF3 does not significantly alter the bond angle from those of the other trihalides.

NF3 has a bond angle of approximately 102.5 degrees. The lone pair of electrons on the central nitrogen (N) atom and the electronegativity of fluorine results in a bond angle smaller than that of NH3.

NBr3 has the smallest bond angle among the options. The bond angle is approximately 101 degrees due to the larger size of bromine (Br) atoms, which increases repulsion and reduces the bond angle more than in NH3 or NF3.

questions.explanation: NF3 has a bond angle of approximately 107 degrees, similar to that of ammonia (NH3). The lone pair of electrons on the central nitrogen (N) atom contributes to the smaller bond angle compared to the ideal tetrahedral angle.

questions.text: Which of the following has distorted tetrahedral structure?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Carbon tetrachloride (CCl4) has a regular tetrahedral structure with no lone pairs on the central carbon atom. The four chlorine atoms are symmetrically arranged, resulting in no distortion.

Arsine (AsH3) has a distorted tetrahedral structure due to the presence of a lone pair on the arsenic atom. This lone pair repels the hydrogen atoms, causing a deviation from the ideal tetrahedral angle, resulting in a pyramidal shape.
Silicon tetrachloride (SiCl4) also has a regular tetrahedral structure similar to CCl4, with no lone pairs on the silicon atom and symmetrically arranged chlorine atoms.
Acetylene (C2H2) has a linear geometry due to the triple bond between the carbon atoms. It does not exhibit a tetrahedral structure, distorted or otherwise.

questions.explanation: Arsine (AsH3) is a molecule containing one arsenic (As) atom and three hydrogen (H) atoms. It has a distorted tetrahedral structure due to the lone pair of electrons on the arsenic atom. This lone pair causes the hydrogen atoms to be pushed slightly closer together, resulting in a slight distortion from a perfect tetrahedral angle. AsH3 exhibits a pyramidal molecular geometry due to the influence of the lone pair.

questions.text: CO2 is iso structural with.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: H2S has a bent shape due to the presence of lone pairs on sulfur, which is not similar to the linear shape of CO2.
SO2 also has a bent shape due to lone pairs on sulfur, which differs from the linear shape of CO2.
CS2 is iso-structural with CO2 as both have a linear structure with similar bond angles.
SnC2 does not exist as a simple diatomic molecule with a linear structure, unlike CO2.

questions.explanation: CO2 is a linear molecule with the central carbon atom forming double bonds with two oxygen atoms. This structure results in a bond angle of 180°, which is characteristic of linear molecules. CS2 is iso-structural with CO2 because it also forms a linear structure with the central carbon atom forming double bonds with two sulfur atoms, maintaining the same bond angle of 180°. In contrast, H2S and SO2 have bent structures due to lone pairs on the sulfur atom, which results in bond angles less than 180°. SnC2 does not exist in a simple diatomic state similar to CO2 and therefore cannot be considered iso-structural.

questions.text: Which one of the followings contains all types of bonds?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: NH4Cl is composed of ammonium ions (NH4+) and chloride ions (Cl-). It includes:Covalent Bonds: Within the ammonium ion, covalent bonds exist between the nitrogen atom and four hydrogen atoms.Ionic Bonds: Ionic interaction occurs between the ammonium ions and chloride ions.Potential Hydrogen Bonding: Possible due to the presence of hydrogen atoms in NH4+ that can form hydrogen bonds.
NaCl consists of sodium ions (Na+) and chloride ions (Cl-), connected by ionic bonds. It lacks covalent and hydrogen bonds within its structure.
HCl is a molecule with a single covalent bond between the hydrogen and chlorine atoms. It does not contain ionic bonds or hydrogen bonding within the molecule itself.
AlCl3 primarily contains covalent bonds between aluminum and chlorine atoms. While it can engage in ionic interactions externally, it lacks ionic and hydrogen bonds within its own structure.

questions.explanation: NH4Cl (Ammonium Chloride) is the one that contains all three types of chemical bonds: covalent, ionic, and potential hydrogen bonding.

questions.text: The number of unpaired electrons in NH3:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Correct. In NH₃, the nitrogen atom forms three bonds with hydrogen and has one lone pair of electrons, resulting in no unpaired electrons.
Incorrect. The two electrons mentioned are paired as a lone pair on the nitrogen atom, not unpaired electrons.
Incorrect. NH₃ does not contain unpaired electrons in its ground state; all valence electrons are paired in bonds or as a lone pair.
Incorrect. There are not four unpaired electrons in NH₃; only one lone pair is present.

questions.explanation: In ammonia (NH₃), the nitrogen atom has five valence electrons, and each of the three hydrogen atoms contributes one valence electron. The Lewis structure of NH₃ shows that nitrogen forms three single bonds with the hydrogen atoms and has one lone pair of electrons. Here's a summary of the electron configuration:Nitrogen (N): 5 valence electronsBonding with Hydrogen (H): 3 electrons used for bonding, 2 electrons remain as a lone pair

questions.text: How many pure covalent bonds are there in NH+4 ion:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The NH+4 ion (ammonium ion) has no pure covalent bonds. All bonds are polar covalent due to the electronegativity difference between nitrogen and hydrogen, causing unequal sharing of electrons.
Incorrect. The ammonium ion does not contain any pure covalent bonds. All bonds are polar covalent.
Incorrect. The ammonium ion cannot have 3 pure covalent bonds as all bonds are polar covalent.
Incorrect. All four bonds in the ammonium ion are polar covalent due to the difference in electronegativity between nitrogen and hydrogen.

questions.explanation: The NH+4 ion (ammonium ion) has no pure covalent bonds. All the bonds within the NH+4 ion are polar covalent bonds. In the ammonium ion, nitrogen (N) is bonded to four hydrogen (H) atoms. Each of these bonds involves the sharing of electrons between the N and H atoms, but due to the electronegativity difference between nitrogen and hydrogen, the electrons are not shared equally. As a result, the bonds are polar covalent, with nitrogen having a partial negative charge and each hydrogen having a partial positive charge.

questions.text: The number of sigma electrons in ethene are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is incorrect. Ethene has more than 5 sigma electrons because each hydrogen and carbon atom forms sigma bonds in its structure.

This is correct. Ethene (C2H4) contains 10 sigma (σ) electrons. Each carbon atom forms three sigma bonds: two with hydrogen atoms and one with the other carbon atom. The remaining electrons are involved in the pi bond.
This is incorrect. Ethene must have more sigma electrons since there are multiple bonds between carbon and hydrogen atoms.
This is incorrect. While ethene has 8 valence electrons, the count for sigma electrons is different, totaling 10.

questions.explanation: Ethene (C2H4) contains 10 sigma (σ) electrons. In the molecular structure of ethene, each carbon (C) atom is connected to three other atoms: two hydrogen (H) atoms and one neighboring carbon atom. The carbon-carbon double bond in ethene consists of one sigma bond (σ) and one pi bond (π). The sigma bond forms from the head-on overlap of sp2 hybrid orbitals from each carbon atom, and the pi bond results from the side-to-side overlap of p orbitals.

questions.text: Point out the shape and bond angles present hi Boron trifluoride:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Trigonal pyramidal, bond angles of approximately 107°
options.explanation: This configuration is typical for molecules with a lone pair affecting the geometry, such as ammonia (NH₃).
This shape is characteristic of molecules like methane (CH₄) with four equivalent bonds around the central atom.
This is the correct configuration for Boron trifluoride (BF₃), which has three equivalent bonds and no lone pairs on the boron atom.
This shape applies to molecules like carbon dioxide (CO₂) with two double bonds around the central atom.

questions.explanation: Boron trifluoride (BF₃) has a trigonal planar shape with bond angles of 120°. This is because the boron atom forms three sigma bonds with fluorine atoms, and there are no lone pairs on the boron atom to alter the geometry. As a result, the molecule adopts a flat, triangular shape with equal bond angles. The other options describe molecular geometries that occur when there are different numbers of bonds and/or lone pairs influencing the geometry: trigonal pyramidal in the presence of a lone pair, tetrahedral with four bonds, and linear with two bonds.

questions.text: _Pair of molecules will have similar shaper
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: SO3 is trigonal planar while PCl3 is trigonal pyramidal due to the presence of a lone pair on the phosphorus atom, which makes their shapes different.
NH3 (ammonia) has a trigonal pyramidal shape due to the lone pair on nitrogen, while CH4 (methane) is tetrahedral, making their shapes different.
CO2 is linear, whereas SO2 is bent due to a lone pair on the sulfur atom, resulting in different shapes.
Both BF3 and SO3 have a trigonal planar shape with no lone pairs on the central atom, making their shapes similar.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D:BF3 and SO3. Both of these molecules adopt a trigonal planar shape. In BF3, the boron atom forms bonds with three fluorine atoms, and there are no lone pairs on the boron, resulting in a planar structure. Similarly, in SO3, the sulfur atom forms bonds with three oxygen atoms, with no lone pairs, also resulting in a trigonal planar shape. The other options differ in shapes due to the presence of lone pairs which alter the molecular geometry.

questions.text: Which bond is formed first between two atoms?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A sigma bond is the initial type of covalent bond formed by the head-on overlap of atomic orbitals such as s-s, s-p, or p-p. Sigma bonds are the strongest covalent bonds and are typically formed first when two atoms come together to form a molecule.

A pi bond arises from the side-to-side overlap of parallel p orbitals. These bonds are weaker than sigma bonds and are formed in addition to sigma bonds in molecules with double or triple bonds. Pi bonds are formed after the sigma bond.

A double bond consists of one sigma and one pi bond between two atoms. Although it includes a sigma bond, it is not the first type of bond formed. The initial bond in a double bond formation is the sigma bond.

A triple bond consists of one sigma bond and two pi bonds. While a triple bond formation starts with a sigma bond, it is not the first type of bond formed between two atoms. The process involves the formation of the sigma bond first, followed by the pi bonds.

questions.explanation: A sigma bond is a type of covalent bond formed by the head-on overlap of atomic orbitals. It's the strongest type of covalent bond and is often formed between two s orbitals a p orbital. Sigma bonds are usually formed first when two atoms approach each other to create a covalent molecule.

questions.text: Which of the following has different molecular geometry and electron pair geometry?
questions.subject: Chemistry
